testcase.name: test_request_not_authorized

failure.message: AssertionError: expected call not found. Expected: request('get', 'https://api.allegion.yonomi.cloud/v1/foo/bar', timeout=60, auth=<MagicMock name='RequestsSrpAuth()' spec='RequestsSrpAuth' id='4406484240'>, headers={'X-Api-Key': 'hnuu9jbbJr7MssFDWm5nU2Z7nG5Q5rxsaqWsE7e9'}, baz='bam') Actual: request('get', 'https://api.allegion.yonomi.cloud/v1/foo/bar', baz='bam', auth=<MagicMock name='RequestsSrpAuth()' spec='RequestsSrpAuth' id='4406484240'>, headers={'X-Api-Key': 'hnuu9jbbJr7MssFDWm5nU2Z7nG5Q5rxsaqWsE7e9'}, timeout=60)

testcase.failure:
TypeError: got an unexpected keyword argument 'baz'
The above exception was the direct cause of the following exception:
mock_cognito = <MagicMock name='Cognito' id='4409581648'> mock_srp_auth = <MagicMock name='RequestsSrpAuth' spec='RequestsSrpAuth' id='4409587472'> mock_request = <MagicMock name='request' spec='function' id='4370530896'>
@mock.patch("httpx.request", spec=True) @mock.patch("pycognito.utils.RequestsSrpAuth", spec=True) @mock.patch("pycognito.Cognito") def test_request_not_authorized(mock_cognito, mock_srp_auth, mock_request): url = "https://api.allegion.yonomi.cloud/v1/foo/bar" auth = _auth.Auth("__username__", "__password__") mock_request.side_effect = ClientError( { "Error": { "Code": "NotAuthorizedException", "Message": f"Unauthorized for url: {url}", } }, "foo-op", )
with pytest.raises( pyschlage.exceptions.NotAuthorizedError, match=f"Unauthorized for url: {url}" ): auth.request("get", "/foo/bar", baz="bam")
> mock_request.assert_called_once_with( "get", url, timeout=60, auth=mock_srp_auth.return_value, headers={"X-Api-Key": _auth.API_KEY}, baz="bam", )
tests/test_auth.py:65: _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ /Library/Frameworks/Python.framework/Versions/3.11/lib/python3.11/unittest/mock.py:951: in assert_called_once_with return self.assert_called_with(*args, **kwargs) ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _
self = <MagicMock name='request' spec='function' id='4370530896'> args = ('get', 'https://api.allegion.yonomi.cloud/v1/foo/bar') kwargs = {'auth': <MagicMock name='RequestsSrpAuth()' spec='RequestsSrpAuth' id='4406484240'>, 'baz': 'bam', 'headers': {'X-Api-Key': 'hnuu9jbbJr7MssFDWm5nU2Z7nG5Q5rxsaqWsE7e9'}, 'timeout': 60} expected = TypeError("got an unexpected keyword argument 'baz'") actual = TypeError("got an unexpected keyword argument 'baz'") _error_message = <function NonCallableMock.assert_called_with.<locals>._error_message at 0x106ade020> cause = TypeError("got an unexpected keyword argument 'baz'")
def assert_called_with(self, /, *args, **kwargs): """assert that the last call was made with the specified arguments.
Raises an AssertionError if the args and keyword args passed in are different to the last call to the mock.""" if self.call_args is None: expected = self._format_mock_call_signature(args, kwargs) actual = 'not called.' error_message = ('expected call not found.\nExpected: %s\nActual: %s' % (expected, actual)) raise AssertionError(error_message)
def _error_message(): msg = self._format_mock_failure_message(args, kwargs) return msg expected = self._call_matcher(_Call((args, kwargs), two=True)) actual = self._call_matcher(self.call_args) if actual != expected: cause = expected if isinstance(expected, Exception) else None > raise AssertionError(_error_message()) from cause E AssertionError: expected call not found. E Expected: request('get', 'https://api.allegion.yonomi.cloud/v1/foo/bar', timeout=60, auth=<MagicMock name='RequestsSrpAuth()' spec='RequestsSrpAuth' id='4406484240'>, headers={'X-Api-Key': 'hnuu9jbbJr7MssFDWm5nU2Z7nG5Q5rxsaqWsE7e9'}, baz='bam') E Actual: request('get', 'https://api.allegion.yonomi.cloud/v1/foo/bar', baz='bam', auth=<MagicMock name='RequestsSrpAuth()' spec='RequestsSrpAuth' id='4406484240'>, headers={'X-Api-Key': 'hnuu9jbbJr7MssFDWm5nU2Z7nG5Q5rxsaqWsE7e9'}, timeout=60)
/Library/Frameworks/Python.framework/Versions/3.11/lib/python3.11/unittest/mock.py:939: AssertionError

testcase.name: test_request_unknown_error

failure.message: TypeError: HTTPStatusError.__init__() missing 2 required keyword-only arguments: 'request' and 'response'

testcase.name: test_to_from_json_temporary_schedule

failure.message:
AssertionError: assert AccessCode(na...isabled=False) == AccessCode(na...isabled=False)
Omitting 10 identical items, use -vv to show Differing attributes: ['schedule']
Drill down into differing attribute schedule: schedule: TemporarySchedule(start=datetime.datetime(2022, 12, 25, 12, 30), end=datetime.datetime(2022, 12, 25, 13, 0)) != TemporarySchedule(start=datetime.datetime(2022, 12, 25, 8, 30), end=datetime.datetime(2022, 12, 25, 9, 0))...
...Full output truncated (9 lines hidden), use '-vv' to show

testcase.failure:
self = <tests.test_code.TestAccessCode object at 0x106ac7150> mock_auth = <MagicMock spec_set='Auth' id='4409282256'> access_code_json = {'accessCode': 123, 'accessCodeLength': 4, 'accesscodeId': '__access_code_uuid__', 'activationSecs': 0, ...} wifi_device = Device(device_id='__wifi_uuid__', device_type='be489wifi')
def test_to_from_json_temporary_schedule( self, mock_auth: Mock, access_code_json: dict[str, Any], wifi_device: Device ): access_code_id = "__access_code_uuid__" sched = TemporarySchedule( start=datetime(2022, 12, 25, 8, 30, 0), end=datetime(2022, 12, 25, 9, 0, 0), ) json = deepcopy(access_code_json) json["activationSecs"] = 1671957000 json["expirationSecs"] = 1671958800 code = AccessCode( _auth=mock_auth, _json=json, _device=wifi_device, name="Friendly name", code="0123", schedule=sched, device_id=wifi_device.device_id, access_code_id=access_code_id, ) > assert AccessCode.from_json(mock_auth, wifi_device, json) == code E AssertionError: assert AccessCode(na...isabled=False) == AccessCode(na...isabled=False) E E Omitting 10 identical items, use -vv to show E Differing attributes: E ['schedule'] E E Drill down into differing attribute schedule: E schedule: TemporarySchedule(start=datetime.datetime(2022, 12, 25, 12, 30), end=datetime.datetime(2022, 12, 25, 13, 0)) != TemporarySchedule(start=datetime.datetime(2022, 12, 25, 8, 30), end=datetime.datetime(2022, 12, 25, 9, 0))... E E ...Full output truncated (9 lines hidden), use '-vv' to show
tests/test_code.py:75: AssertionError

testcase.name: test_lock_ble

failure.message: AttributeError: Mock object has no attribute 'post'

testcase.failure:
self = <tests.test_lock.TestLock object at 0x106aebc50> mock_auth = <MagicMock spec_set='Auth' id='4414826192'> ble_lock_json = {'CAT': 'abcdef', 'SAT': 'ghijkl', 'attributes': {'CAT': 'abcdef', 'SAT': 'ghijkl', 'accessCodeLength': 4, 'adminOnlyEnabled': 0, ...}, 'connected': False, ...}
def test_lock_ble(self, mock_auth, ble_lock_json): with pytest.raises(NotAuthenticatedError): Lock().lock()
lock = Lock.from_json(mock_auth, ble_lock_json) > lock.lock()
tests/test_lock.py:214: _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ pyschlage/lock.py:235: in lock self._toggle(1) pyschlage/lock.py:225: in _toggle self.send_command("changelockstate", data) pyschlage/device.py:67: in send_command return self._auth.post(path, json=json) # Changed request method to post ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _
self = <MagicMock spec_set='Auth' id='4414826192'>, name = 'post'
def __getattr__(self, name): if name in {'_mock_methods', '_mock_unsafe'}: raise AttributeError(name) elif self._mock_methods is not None: if name not in self._mock_methods or name in _all_magics: > raise AttributeError("Mock object has no attribute %r" % name) E AttributeError: Mock object has no attribute 'post'
/Library/Frameworks/Python.framework/Versions/3.11/lib/python3.11/unittest/mock.py:653: AttributeError

testcase.name: test_unlock_ble

failure.message: AttributeError: Mock object has no attribute 'post'

testcase.failure:
self = <tests.test_lock.TestLock object at 0x106af8050> mock_auth = <MagicMock spec_set='Auth' id='4407283024'> ble_lock_json = {'CAT': 'abcdef', 'SAT': 'ghijkl', 'attributes': {'CAT': 'abcdef', 'SAT': 'ghijkl', 'accessCodeLength': 4, 'adminOnlyEnabled': 0, ...}, 'connected': False, ...}
def test_unlock_ble(self, mock_auth, ble_lock_json): with pytest.raises(NotAuthenticatedError): Lock().unlock()
lock = Lock.from_json(mock_auth, ble_lock_json) > lock.unlock()

testcase.name: test_add_access_code

failure.message: AttributeError: Mock object has no attribute 'post'

testcase.failure:
self = <tests.test_lock.TestLock object at 0x106af9310> mock_auth = <MagicMock spec_set='Auth' id='4410079056'> lock_json = {'CAT': '01234', 'SAT': '98765', 'attributes': {'CAT': '01234', 'SAT': '98765', 'accessCodeLength': 4, 'actAlarmBuzzerEnabled': 0, ...}, 'connected': True, ...} access_code_json = {'accessCode': 123, 'accessCodeLength': 4, 'accesscodeId': '__access_code_uuid__', 'activationSecs': 0, ...} notification_json = {'active': False, 'createdAt': '2023-03-01T17:26:47.366Z', 'deviceId': '__wifi_uuid__', 'devicetypeId': 'be489wifi', ...}
def test_add_access_code( self, mock_auth: Mock, lock_json: dict[str, Any], access_code_json: dict[str, Any], notification_json: dict[str, Any], ): lock = Lock.from_json(mock_auth, lock_json) code = AccessCode.from_json(mock_auth, lock, access_code_json) # Users should not set these. code._auth = None code._device = None code.access_code_id = None code.device_id = None json = code.to_json()
notification_json["active"] = False mock_auth.request.side_effect = [ Mock(json=Mock(return_value=access_code_json)), Mock(json=Mock(return_value=notification_json)), ] > lock.add_access_code(code)
tests/test_lock.py:327: _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ pyschlage/lock.py:372: in add_access_code code.save() pyschlage/code.py:249: in save resp = self._device.send_command(command, self.to_json()) ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^ pyschlage/device.py:67: in send_command return self._auth.post(path, json=json) # Changed request method to post ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _
self = <MagicMock spec_set='Auth' id='4410079056'>, name = 'post'
def __getattr__(self, name): if name in {'_mock_methods', '_mock_unsafe'}: raise AttributeError(name) elif self._mock_methods is not None: if name not in self._mock_methods or name in _all_magics: > raise AttributeError("Mock object has no attribute %r" % name) E AttributeError: Mock object has no attribute 'post'
/Library/Frameworks/Python.framework/Versions/3.11/lib/python3.11/unittest/mock.py:653: AttributeError

testcase.name: test_unlocked_by_thumbturn

failure.message:
AssertionError: assert LockLog(creat..._code_id=None) == LockLog(creat..._code_id=None)
Omitting 3 identical items, use -vv to show Differing attributes: ['created_at']
Drill down into differing attribute created_at: created_at: datetime.datetime(2023, 3, 1, 21, 26, 47, 366000) != datetime.datetime(2023, 3, 1, 17, 26, 47, 366000)
